title: Aureline trust-root continuity and rotation state record
description: Boundary schema for the trust_root_rotation_state_record frozen in docs/release/artifact_verification_contract.md. The record carries one trust-root family identity, one current-root descriptor (root id, root kind, validity window, fingerprint ref), the supersedes / revokes linkage to predecessor roots, the rollout epoch class, the overlap posture (dual-signed, cross-signed, single-signed pre or post rotation, no overlap emergency), the continuity statement (signed continuity, cross-signed transition, exception record, or emergency posture), the stale-or-rotating state class, and the per-surface projection set release / update / install / About / support / mirror surfaces quote when a verification row binds to a rotation state. Raw key material, raw signature bytes, raw transparency-log payloads, raw rotation policy bodies, raw URLs, raw private trust-root endpoints, and raw signing infrastructure identifiers never cross this boundary; records carry opaque refs, typed states, and bounded reviewable summaries only. The vocabularies are aligned with /docs/release/supply_chain_trust_framework_matrix.md (artifact family group, trust-root rotation policy), /artifacts/release/trust_framework_rows.yaml, /schemas/governance/provenance_badge.schema.json (trust_root_state), and /schemas/release/artifact_verification_row.schema.json (trust_root_pointer_class) so a rotation state composes onto the existing trust-framework matrix and the verification row instead of minting a second rotation language. Adding a new trust_root_family_class, root_kind_class, rollout_epoch_class, overlap_posture_class, continuity_statement_class, stale_or_rotating_state_class, surface_class, projection_mode_class, redaction_class, or denial_reason_class is additive-minor and bumps trust_root_rotation_state_schema_version; repurposing an existing value, weakening any allOf invariant, or collapsing two refusal states into one bare label is breaking and requires a new decision row.

trust_root_rotation_state_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the trust-root rotation state payload shape. Current value is 1.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable identifier safe for live surfaces, support bundles, public-proof packets, diagnostics exports, release packets, install review sheets, and offline review. Raw URLs, raw key material, raw signing infrastructure identifiers, and raw transparency-log payloads never appear here.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewable sentence. Raw key material, raw signatures, raw transparency-log payloads, raw rotation policy bodies, raw URLs, and raw private trust-root endpoints MUST NOT appear.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures; never part of the runtime record.

trust_root_family_class.description: Closed trust-root-family vocabulary mirroring the artifact-family groups in /docs/release/supply_chain_trust_framework_matrix.md plus the mirror_continuity_root that anchors mirror-snapshot continuity. Adding a new family is additive-minor and bumps schema version.

root_kind_class.description: Closed root-kind vocabulary aligned with the trust patterns named in /docs/release/supply_chain_trust_framework_matrix.md (TUF-style metadata, Sigstore-style identity, platform-native signing, SLSA-style provenance, OCI Distribution-compatible mirror transport).

rollout_epoch_class.description: Closed rollout-epoch vocabulary. rollout_planned is the published-but-not-yet-active state. rollout_active_overlap_period is the overlap window during which the predecessor and current root both validate. rollout_overlap_ended is the post-overlap state where only the current root validates. rollout_completed_legacy_grace is the named grace window during which legacy artifacts signed under the predecessor root are still admitted (read-only) for inspection but not for new installs. rollout_revoked is the explicit refusal state when a root rotation has been revoked. rollout_emergency is the emergency-rotation state when the predecessor root has been compromised and the rotation shipped without a planned overlap.

overlap_posture_class.description: Closed overlap-posture vocabulary. dual_signed_overlap names the case where the artifact carries signatures under both the predecessor and the current root during the overlap window. cross_signed_overlap names the case where the current root is cross-signed by the predecessor root. single_signed_pre_rotation and single_signed_post_rotation name the two single-signed boundary states. no_overlap_emergency and no_overlap_planned_legacy_grace_only are the two no-overlap states; collapsing them into one chip is non-conforming because the support-review path differs.

continuity_statement_class.description: Closed continuity-statement vocabulary. signed_continuity_statement names the standard case where the predecessor root signs a statement that delegates trust to the current root. cross_signed_transition names the case where the current root is cross-signed by the predecessor root. exception_record names the case where a /schemas/release/provenance_stack_exception.schema.json record stands in for a continuity statement that could not be issued. no_continuity_emergency names the explicit emergency case where the predecessor root was compromised and no continuity statement is admissible. not_applicable_initial_root names the case where the rotation record describes the first issuance of a trust root family (no predecessor).

stale_or_rotating_state_class.description: Closed stale-or-rotating-state vocabulary that the verification row's trust_root_pointer_class projects against verbatim. trust_root_stale names the case where the rotation record exists but the rotation has not been reviewed by its review forum within the named freshness floor; collapsing it with trust_root_unknown is non-conforming because the support-review path differs.

root_id.description: Stable trust-root id. Quoted by verification rows, release-evidence packets, About panels, support exports, mirror-continuity records, and provenance-badge records.

valid_until.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp at which this root expires. Null is admissible only when the rollout_epoch_class is rollout_emergency or rollout_revoked (no scheduled expiry; the root is held under emergency posture or revoked).

fingerprint_ref.description: Stable ref into the fingerprint catalog so reviewers can pivot to the canonical fingerprint without restating the fingerprint bytes here.

issuer_ref.description: Stable ref into the issuer record (release council, security council, or external CA). Raw issuer URLs and raw issuer DNs are forbidden.

trust_framework_row_ref.description: Stable ref into /artifacts/release/trust_framework_rows.yaml so the trust pattern this root follows is reviewable.

supersedes_root_refs.description: Stable refs into predecessor trust-root rotation state records that this root supersedes. Empty only when the continuity_statement_class is not_applicable_initial_root.

revokes_root_refs.description: Stable refs into predecessor trust-root rotation state records that this root explicitly revokes. Required to be non-empty when the rollout_epoch_class is rollout_revoked or rollout_emergency.

successor_root_ref.description: Stable ref into a successor trust-root rotation state record. Required when the rollout_epoch_class is rollout_completed_legacy_grace, rollout_overlap_ended, or rollout_revoked; null otherwise.

linked_provenance_stack_exception_ref.description: Stable ref into /schemas/release/provenance_stack_exception.schema.json. Required when continuity_statement_class is exception_record; null otherwise.

rotation_decision_row_ref.description: Stable ref into /artifacts/governance/decision_index.yaml so the rotation is reviewable as an architecture / security decision row.

rotation_review_forum_ref.description: Stable ref into the review-forum row that governs this rotation (release council, security council, shiproom, etc.).

continuity_statement_ref.description: Stable ref into the signed continuity statement, cross-signed transition record, or exception record. Required when continuity_statement_class is signed_continuity_statement, cross_signed_transition, or exception_record; null when continuity_statement_class is no_continuity_emergency or not_applicable_initial_root.

overlap_starts_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp at which the overlap window starts. Required when overlap_posture_class is dual_signed_overlap or cross_signed_overlap; null otherwise.

overlap_ends_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp at which the overlap window ends. Required when overlap_posture_class is dual_signed_overlap or cross_signed_overlap; null otherwise.

legacy_grace_until.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp at which legacy-grace inspection ends. Required when rollout_epoch_class is rollout_completed_legacy_grace or stale_or_rotating_state_class is trust_root_post_rotation_legacy_grace; null otherwise.

next_review_due_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp at which the rotation MUST be re-reviewed. Null only when stale_or_rotating_state_class is trust_root_unknown or rollout_epoch_class is rollout_revoked.

raw_private_material_excluded.description: MUST be true. Raw key material, raw signatures, raw transparency-log payloads, raw rotation policy bodies, raw URLs, raw private trust-root endpoints, and raw signing infrastructure identifiers never cross this boundary.

surface_projections.description: Set of surface projections this rotation state MUST render onto. The four required floor surfaces (release_publication, update_center, install_review_sheet, about_provenance_panel) are mandatory; tooling MAY add more, but MUST NOT drop one.

allOf.description: rollout_planned rotations MUST resolve to single_signed_pre_rotation overlap posture, the trust_root_rotation_pending_review stale-or-rotating state, a non-null rollout_started_at, a null rollout_overlap_ended_at, and a null rollout_completed_at. The continuity statement MUST be signed_continuity_statement or cross_signed_transition; emergency or exception postures are forbidden because the rotation has not yet started.

rollout_active_overlap_period rotations MUST resolve to dual_signed_overlap or cross_signed_overlap overlap posture, a non-null overlap_starts_at, a non-null overlap_ends_at, and a stale_or_rotating_state_class of trust_root_rotating_dual_signed or trust_root_rotating_cross_signed.
rollout_overlap_ended rotations MUST resolve to single_signed_post_rotation overlap posture, the trust_root_rotated_with_continuity stale-or-rotating state, and a non-null rollout_overlap_ended_at.

rollout_completed_legacy_grace rotations MUST resolve to no_overlap_planned_legacy_grace_only overlap posture, the trust_root_post_rotation_legacy_grace stale-or-rotating state, a non-null legacy_grace_until, and a non-null rollout_completed_at.
rollout_revoked rotations MUST resolve to the trust_root_revoked stale-or-rotating state, a non-empty revokes_root_refs array, and a non-null successor_root_ref.

rollout_emergency rotations MUST resolve to no_overlap_emergency overlap posture, no_continuity_emergency continuity statement (with a null continuity_statement_ref), and a non-empty revokes_root_refs array. Emergency rotations cannot present a planned overlap because the predecessor root was compromised.

Continuity statement class signed_continuity_statement, cross_signed_transition, and exception_record MUST resolve to a non-null continuity_statement_ref. exception_record additionally requires a non-null linked_provenance_stack_exception_ref.
exception_record continuity statement requires a non-null continuity_statement_ref AND a non-null linked_provenance_stack_exception_ref.
not_applicable_initial_root continuity statement MUST resolve to an empty supersedes_root_refs array. The initial issuance has no predecessor.

Required surface projection floor: release_publication, update_center, install_review_sheet, about_provenance_panel. Tooling MAY add support_bundle, public_proof_packet, mirror_offline_review, marketplace_discovery, and post_install_disclosure as secondary surfaces.

The export envelope MUST set raw_private_material_excluded to true. Raw key material, raw signatures, raw transparency-log payloads, raw rotation policy bodies, raw URLs, raw private trust-root endpoints, and raw signing infrastructure identifiers never cross this boundary.
